children.label: i
children.text: has a response program that includes each of the elements, or is taking reasonable steps to include each of the elements, listed in paragraph (2); or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a party to a memorandum of agreement with the Administrator for voluntary response programs.

children.heading: Use of grants by States
children.label: B Use of grants by States

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A State or Indian tribe may use a grant under this subsection to establish or enhance the response program of the State or Indian tribe.

children.label: I
children.text: capitalize a revolving loan fund for brownfield remediation under ;section 9604(k)(3) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: purchase insurance or develop a risk sharing pool, an indemnity pool, or insurance mechanism to provide financing for response actions under a State response program; or

children.label: III
children.text: assist small communities, Indian tribes, rural areas, or disadvantaged areas in carrying out activities described in with respect to brownfield sites.section 9604(k)(7)(A) of this title

children.heading: Small communities, Indian tribes, rural areas, and disadvantaged areas
children.label: iii Small communities, Indian tribes, rural areas, and disadvantaged areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: To make grants to States or Indian tribes under clause (ii)(III), the Administrator may use, in addition to amounts available to carry out this subsection, not more than $1,500,000 of the amounts made available to carry out in each fiscal year.section 9604(k)(7) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: II Limitation
children.text: Each grant made under subclause (I) may be not more than $20,000.

children.heading: Inclusion in other grants
children.label: III Inclusion in other grants
children.text: The Administrator may, at the request of a State or Indian tribe, include a grant under this clause in any other grant to the State or Indian tribe made under this subsection.

children.heading: Disadvantaged area
children.label: I Disadvantaged area
children.text: The term “disadvantaged area” means a community with an annual median household income that is less than 80 percent of the statewide annual median household income, as determined by the President based on the latest available decennial census.

children.heading: Small community
children.label: II Small community
children.text: The term “small community” means a community with a population of not more than 15,000 individuals, as determined by the President based on the latest available decennial census.

children.label: A
children.text: Timely survey and inventory of brownfield sites in the State.

children.label: I
children.text: protect human health and the environment; and

children.label: II
children.text: be conducted in accordance with applicable Federal and State law; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the person conducting the response action fails to complete the necessary response activities, including operation and maintenance or long-term monitoring activities, the necessary response activities are completed.

children.label: i
children.text: public access to documents that the State, Indian tribe, or party conducting the cleanup is relying on or developing in making cleanup decisions or conducting site activities;

children.label: ii
children.text: prior notice and opportunity for comment on proposed cleanup plans and site activities; and

children.label: I
children.text: a person that is or may be affected by a release or threatened release of a hazardous substance, pollutant, or contaminant at a brownfield site located in the community in which the person works or resides may request the conduct of a site assessment; and

children.label: II
children.text: an appropriate State official shall consider and appropriately respond to a request under subclause (I).

children.label: D
children.text: Mechanisms for approval of a cleanup plan, and a requirement for verification by and certification or similar documentation from the State, an Indian tribe, or a licensed site professional to the person conducting a response action indicating that the response is complete.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Enforcement in cases of a release subject to State program
children.label: b Enforcement in cases of a release subject to State program
i
children.text: there is a release or threatened release of a hazardous substance, pollutant, or contaminant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a person is conducting or has completed a response action regarding the specific release that is addressed by the response action that is in compliance with the State program that specifically governs response actions for the protection of public health and the environment,

children.label: i
children.text: the State requests that the President provide assistance in the performance of a response action;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Administrator determines that contamination has migrated or will migrate across a State line, resulting in the need for further response action to protect human health or the environment, or the President determines that contamination has migrated or is likely to migrate onto property subject to the jurisdiction, custody, or control of a department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States and may impact the authorized purposes of the Federal property;

children.label: I
children.text: a release or threatened release may present an imminent and substantial endangerment to public health or welfare or the environment; and

children.label: II
children.text: additional response actions are likely to be necessary to address, prevent, limit, or mitigate the release or threatened release; or

children.label: iv
children.text: the Administrator, after consultation with the State, determines that information, that on the earlier of the date on which cleanup was approved or completed, was not known by the State, as recorded in documents prepared or relied on in selecting or conducting the cleanup, has been discovered regarding the contamination or conditions at a facility such that the contamination or conditions at the facility present a threat requiring further remediation to protect public health or welfare or the environment. Consultation with the State shall not limit the ability of the Administrator to make this determination.

children.heading: Public record
children.label: C Public record
children.text: The limitations on the authority of the President under subparagraph (A) apply only at sites in States that maintain, update not less than annually, and make available to the public a record of sites, by name and location, at which response actions have been completed in the previous year and are planned to be addressed under the State program that specifically governs response actions for the protection of public health and the environment in the upcoming year. The public record shall identify whether or not the site, on completion of the response action, will be suitable for unrestricted use and, if not, shall identify the institutional controls relied on in the remedy. Each State and tribe receiving financial assistance under subsection (a) shall maintain and make available to the public a record of sites as provided in this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: notify the State of the action the Administrator intends to take; and

children.label: aa
children.text: wait 48 hours for a reply from the State under clause (ii); or

children.label: bb
children.text: if the State fails to reply to the notification or if the Administrator makes a determination under clause (iii), take immediate action under that clause.

children.label: I
children.text: the release at the eligible response site is or has been subject to a cleanup conducted under a State program; and

children.label: II
children.text: the State is planning to abate the release or threatened release, any actions that are planned.

children.heading: Immediate Federal action
children.label: iii Immediate Federal action
children.text: The Administrator may take action immediately after giving notification under clause (i) without waiting for a State reply under clause (ii) if the Administrator determines that one or more exceptions under subparagraph (B) are met.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: E Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of initiation of any enforcement action by the President under clause (ii), (iii), or (iv) of subparagraph (B), the President shall submit to Congress a report describing the basis for the enforcement action, including specific references to the facts demonstrating that enforcement action is permitted under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Costs incurred prior to limitations
children.label: A Costs incurred prior to limitations
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) precludes the President from seeking to recover costs incurred prior to , or during a period in which the limitations of paragraph (1)(A) were not applicable.January 11, 2002

children.heading: Effect on agreements between States and EPA
children.label: B Effect on agreements between States and EPA
i
children.text: modifies or otherwise affects a memorandum of agreement, memorandum of understanding, or any similar agreement relating to this chapter between a State agency or an Indian tribe and the Administrator that is in effect on or before (which agreement shall remain in effect, subject to the terms of the agreement); orJanuary 11, 2002

children.label: ii
children.text: limits the discretionary authority of the President to enter into or modify an agreement with a State, an Indian tribe, or any other person relating to the implementation by the President of statutory authorities.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 3 Effective date
children.text: This subsection applies only to response actions conducted after .February 15, 2001
this chapter, except as provided in subsection (b);

the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ( et seq.);33 U.S.C. 1251
